message:
fix(node): monotonic audit entry IDs and path traversal rejection in supply chain manifests

Two independent security hardening changes:

atc_reciprocity.rs — Monotonic audit entry IDs:
- Add next_audit_sequence (u64) and audit_epoch (u64) fields to
  ReciprocityEngine to generate strictly monotonic entry IDs
- Replace index-based "audit-{len+1}" IDs with
  "audit-{epoch:016x}-{seq:016x}" format via allocate_audit_entry_id()
- When sequence reaches u64::MAX, wrap to 1 and increment epoch
  (handles the theoretical case of more than 2^64 entries)
- Previously, log eviction (push_bounded) could cause duplicate entry
  IDs since the ID was derived from current log length; after eviction,
  new entries would reuse IDs of evicted entries
- Add audit_entry_ids_remain_unique_after_log_eviction test that creates
  MAX_AUDIT_LOG_ENTRIES + 3 entries and verifies all surviving IDs are
  unique with no collisions

artifact_signing.rs — Path traversal rejection in manifest parsing:
- Harden ChecksumManifest::parse_canonical() to reject entries whose
  name contains path traversal sequences: ".." components, leading "/"
  (absolute paths), or backslash characters
- Attacker-controlled manifests could previously reference arbitrary
  file paths like "../../../etc/passwd" which downstream consumers
  might use for file reads
- Skip malicious entries silently (continue) rather than erroring, so
  a single bad entry doesn't invalidate the entire manifest
- Add parse_canonical_rejects_path_traversal test (../ sequences)
- Add parse_canonical_rejects_absolute_path test (leading /)
- Add parse_canonical_rejects_backslash_path test (Windows-style)
- Add parse_canonical_accepts_clean_names test (nested paths are fine)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
